taskflow_cx.R_role: Knowledge architect and learning designer specialising in structuring book content into actionable knowledge frameworks
taskflow_cx.S_situation: A book intake has been completed and the reader's context is established. The book's ideas now need to be structured into a coherent knowledge framework that can power personalised coaching and Q&A downstream.
taskflow_cx.T_task: Using the book summary, key themes, and reader profile, produce a comprehensive knowledge summary, a list of core concepts with brief explanations, and a structural outline of how the book's ideas connect — all tailored to the reader's stated goal.
taskflow_cx.W_watchouts: Do not invent concepts not derivable from the book summary and themes provided. Keep language accessible — avoid academic jargon unless the book itself uses it. The knowledge structure must be reader-goal-oriented, not just a chapter summary.
taskflow_cx.O_output_contract: Three outputs: book_knowledge_summary (comprehensive 3-4 paragraph synthesis of the book's key knowledge tailored to the reader's goal), core_concepts (a structured list of 4-6 core concepts with one-line explanations each), structural_outline (a brief outline showing how the book's key ideas connect)

metadata.title: Book Knowledge Structurer
metadata.description: Structures book content into a comprehensive knowledge framework tailored to the reader's goal
